Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient registered in the HealthConnect AI application, storing their profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the patient.

lastName.description: The last name of the patient.

email.description: The email address of the patient, used for communication.

dateOfBirth.description: The patient's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format.

contactNumber.description: The patient's primary contact phone number.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the patient's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the patient record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the patient record was last updated.

Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Represents a healthcare institution listed in the application, providing location and contact details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hospital entity.

name.description: The official name of the hospital.

address.description: The street address of the hospital.

city.description: The city where the hospital is located.

state.description: The state or province where the hospital is located.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the hospital's location.

phoneNumber.description: The main contact phone number for the hospital.

websiteUrl.description: The official website URL of the hospital.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude of the hospital's location for mapping purposes.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude of the hospital's location for mapping purposes.

description.description: A brief description or mission statement of the hospital.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the hospital.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a medical doctor available for consultation in the application, including their professional details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the doctor.

lastName.description: The last name of the doctor.

email.description: The professional email address of the doctor.

phoneNumber.description: The professional contact phone number for the doctor.

specialties.description: A list of medical specialties the doctor practices.

licenseNumber.description: The medical license number of the doctor.

experienceYears.description: The total years of professional experience the doctor has.

bio.description: A biographical summary or professional statement of the doctor.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the doctor's professional profile picture.

hospitalIds.description: References to Hospitals where the doctor practices. (Relationship: Doctor N:N Hospital)

averageRating.description: The aggregated average rating from patient reviews.

availableForRemoteConsultation.description: Indicates if the doctor offers remote consultation services.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the doctor record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the doctor record was last updated.

SymptomAnalysis.title: SymptomAnalysis
SymptomAnalysis.description: Stores the results of an AI symptom analysis performed by a patient, including their input and AI's recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SymptomAnalysis entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who performed the analysis. (Relationship: Patient 1:N SymptomAnalysis)

symptomsInput.description: The raw text input of symptoms provided by the patient to the AI.

aiResponse.description: The comprehensive analysis and recommendations generated by the AI chatbot.

potentialConditions.description: A list of potential medical conditions suggested by the AI based on symptoms.

recommendedSpecialties.description: A list of recommended medical specialties for consultation, suggested by the AI.

analysisDateTime.description: Timestamp when the symptom analysis was performed.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a secure messaging thread between a patient and a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient participating in the conversation. (Relationship: Patient 1:N Conversation)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor participating in the conversation. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Conversation)

subject.description: A brief subject or topic for the conversation.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was initiated.

lastMessageAt.description: Timestamp of the last message sent in the conversation.

isActive.description: Indicates if the conversation is currently active or has been closed/archived.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within a conversation between a patient and a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: The ID of the user (Patient or Doctor) who sent the message.

senderType.description: The role of the sender (e.g., 'patient' or 'doctor').

content.description: The textual content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

readAt.description: Timestamp when the message was marked as read by the recipient.

definition.description: Stores individual patient profiles. The `patientId` parameter is directly linked to the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring path-based ownership for secure access. This collection primarily serves as the root for all patient-specific, private data.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient, which matches the Firebase Authentication UID of the logged-in user.

definition.description: Stores the results of AI symptom analyses performed by a specific patient. This subcollection structure ensures that symptom analyses are privately owned by the patient, and access is secured via the parent `patientId` in the path. Includes denormalized 'patientId' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient who owns this symptom analysis, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific symptom analysis record.

definition.description: Contains publicly available information about healthcare institutions. This collection is globally readable to allow all users to discover hospitals without specific authentication or authorization checks, supporting efficient list queries (QAPs).

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific hospital.

definition.description: Contains publicly available professional details of medical doctors. This collection is globally readable to enable patients to search and discover doctors, supporting efficient list queries (QAPs).

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific doctor.

definition.description: Represents a secure messaging thread between a patient and a doctor. Documents in this collection include `patientId` and `doctorId` fields, which are denormalized for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks against `request.auth.uid` to determine access for both participants (QAPs).

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific conversation thread.

definition.description: Contains individual messages within a conversation. Each message document explicitly includes denormalized `patientId` and `doctorId` fields (copied from the parent conversation) to ensure Authorization Independence. This allows security rules to evaluate access to messages atomically without requiring a `get()` call to the parent `Conversation` document, supporting robust QAPs for message listing.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation thread.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific message within the conversation.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for HealthConnect AI prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust security rules and efficient querying. Patients, as core users, have their profiles stored in a top-level collection `/patients/{patientId}`, where `patientId` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, establishing clear ownership. All patient-specific data, such as `SymptomAnalysis` records, are stored as subcollections under the respective patient's path (`/patients/{patientId}/symptomAnalyses/{analysisId}`). This path-based ownership simplifies security rules by allowing direct comparison of `request.auth.uid` with the wildcard `patientId`, eliminating the need for `get()` calls.

Publicly accessible data like `Hospital` and `Doctor` profiles are stored in top-level collections (`/hospitals/{hospitalId}` and `/doctors/{doctorId}`). These collections have a homogeneous security posture, allowing public read access and simplifying their respective rules.

For collaborative data, such as `Conversation` and `Message` entities, the design leverages denormalization for authorization independence. Conversations are stored in a top-level collection `/conversations/{conversationId}`. Each `Conversation` document explicitly includes `patientId` and `doctorId` fields. This is crucial for securing access; a user (either a patient or a doctor) can only access a conversation if their `request.auth.uid` matches either the `patientId` or `doctorId` within the document. This avoids `get()` calls to other user profiles.

Critically, `Message` documents, which are subcollections of `Conversation` (`/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`), also denormalize the `patientId` and `doctorId` from their parent `Conversation` into each `Message` document. This denormalization is essential for Authorization Independence (Mandate A), as it allows security rules for `Message` documents to be evaluated atomically without needing to `get()` the parent `Conversation` document. This enables robust `list` operations (QAPs), as rules can directly check the `patientId` or `doctorId` fields within each message document against `request.auth.uid` for authorization, ensuring that a user can only list messages within conversations they are a part of, and the filtering can be applied at the database level where appropriate.

Overall, this structure provides clear, explicit authorization intent, supports QAPs for listing operations by embedding authorization context directly within documents or deriving it from secure paths, and adheres strictly to the principles of Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.
